title: Quiz: The Phase Estimation Problem
description: Test your understanding of the phase estimation problem

questions.question: If U|ψ⟩ = e^(2πiφ)|ψ⟩, what can we say about the magnitude of the eigenvalue?
questions.explanation: All eigenvalues of unitary matrices have magnitude 1. |e^(iθ)| = 1 for any real θ.

questions.question: Why is phase estimation important for quantum chemistry?

options.text: Eigenvalues of the Hamiltonian give energy levels

questions.explanation: The eigenvalues of the molecular Hamiltonian correspond to energy levels. QPE can find these eigenvalues, enabling ground state energy calculations.

questions.question: Classical phase estimation to n bits of precision requires approximately:
questions.explanation: Classical approaches need exponential 2^n resources, while quantum QPE needs only O(n) controlled-U operations.

questions.question: The phase φ in U|ψ⟩ = e^(2πiφ)|ψ⟩ is always in the range [0, 1).
questions.explanation: By convention, we express phases as φ ∈ [0, 1), representing the fraction of a full rotation.
